title: Say Goodbye to Swollen Gums with These 9 Herbal Creams

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin is good for swollen gums because it has strong anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in the gums by blocking the production of chemical messengers that cause inflammation.
Polyphenols are good for swollen gums because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain, promoting healthy gum tissue and preventing gum disease.
Cichoric acid is good for swollen gums because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and fight off the bacteria that cause gum infections.
Flavonoids are good for swollen gums because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and fight off infections that can cause gum inflammation.
Caffeic acid is good for swollen gums because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and alleviate pain in the gums.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea creams are good for swollen gums because they have anti-inflammatory properties. This means they can help reduce swelling and pain. The active compounds in the cream, such as alkylamides and phenolic acids, work together to calm down the inflammation. When gums are swollen, it can be painful to eat, speak, or even smile. Echinacea purpurea creams can help alleviate this discomfort by reducing inflammation and promoting healing. The cream can also help to soothe the gums and prevent further irritation. Additionally, Echinacea purpurea has antimicrobial properties, which can help prevent infections that can cause swollen gums. By reducing inflammation and preventing infections, Echinacea purpurea creams can help to promote healthy gums and a healthy mouth.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Ginkgo biloba herbal cream for swollen gums, you will need dried Ginkgo biloba leaves, a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil, beeswax, and a few simple kitchen tools. First, dry the Ginkgo biloba leaves by placing them in a low oven (150-200°F) for a few hours or by air drying them. Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle. Then, mix 1-2 tablespoons of the ground Ginkgo biloba powder with 1/4 cup of the carrier oil in a small bowl. Next, melt 2-3 tablespoons of beeswax in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl in short intervals. Once the beeswax has melted, slowly add the Ginkgo biloba mixture and stir until it's well combined. Pour the mixture into a small tin or jar, let it cool, and it's ready to use. Apply a thin layer of the cream to your swollen gums 2-3 times a day to help reduce inflammation and promote healing.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal creams for swollen gums

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Anthraquinones are good for swollen gums because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and ease pain in the gums.
Flavonoids are good for swollen gums because they have strong anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and ease pain in the gums.
Aloin helps swollen gums by reducing inflammation and pain due to its antioxidant properties that soothe and calm irritated gum tissues.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis cream is good for swollen gums because it has anti-inflammatory properties. This means it can help reduce swelling and pain in the gums. The cream is made from the gel inside the aloe plant, which is rich in vitamins and minerals. These nutrients, especially vitamin E, help soothe and calm irritated gums. Aloe barbadensis cream also has antibacterial properties that fight the bacteria that cause gum infections. When applied to swollen gums, it can help reduce redness, swelling, and pain, making it easier to eat and speak. It's also gentle and non-irritating, making it suitable for people with sensitive teeth and gums.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Ginkgo biloba herbal creams that help with swollen gums, you'll need dried Ginkgo biloba leaves, a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil, beeswax, and a clean glass jar. First, mix 1-2 tablespoons of dried Ginkgo biloba leaves with 2-3 tablespoons of carrier oil in a small bowl, and let it sit for 2-3 weeks in a cool, dark place, shaking the mixture every few days. This is called 'infusion' and it helps release the beneficial properties of the Ginkgo biloba leaves into the oil. After the infusion is complete, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another bowl, discarding the solids. Next, melt 1-2 tablespoons of beeswax in a double boiler or in a microwave-safe bowl in short intervals, until it's completely melted. Then, slowly add the infused oil to the melted beeswax, stirring constantly until it's well combined. Pour the mixture into a clean glass jar and let it cool and harden. Once hardened, your Ginkgo biloba herbal cream is ready to use. Simply apply a small amount to your swollen gums and gently massage it in to help reduce inflammation and promote healing.
remedies.image_alt: Aloe barbadensis herbal creams for swollen gums

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Catechins are good for swollen gums because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and fight off the bacteria that cause gum infections.

Tannins are good for swollen gums because they help reduce inflammation and fight bacteria, which can cause pain and swelling in the gums, by acting as a natural antiseptic and anti-inflammatory agent.

Quercetin is good for swollen gums because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and fight off infection, making it an effective natural remedy to soothe and heal gum inflammation.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia, also known as tea tree oil, is a natural ingredient found in some creams. It has antibacterial properties, which help fight off germs that can cause gum infections. When gums become swollen, it can be painful and uncomfortable. The antibacterial properties in Melaleuca alternifolia can help reduce swelling by fighting off the root cause of the infection. This can help soothe and calm the affected area, making it easier to manage pain and discomfort. Additionally, Melaleuca alternifolia has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and redness. When applied topically, it can penetrate deep into the gum tissue, providing relief from swelling and inflammation. This can help improve gum health and reduce the risk of further infection.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Ginkgo biloba herbal cream that helps with swollen gums, start by preparing the Ginkgo biloba extract. You can buy dried Ginkgo biloba leaves or extract powder from a health food store. If using dried leaves, grind them into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle. Next, mix 10 grams of the Ginkgo biloba powder with 50 grams of coconut oil or olive oil in a small saucepan over low heat, stirring constantly until the mixture is well combined. Remove the saucepan from the heat and let it cool. Once cooled, add 10 grams of beeswax to the mixture and stir until it's fully melted and combined. Transfer the mixture to a clean glass jar and let it cool and solidify completely. Once solidified, the Ginkgo biloba cream is ready to use. Apply a small amount to the affected area, gently massaging it in to help reduce swelling and soothe the gums.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis cream is a helpful product for swollen gums. This is because it has anti-inflammatory properties, which means it can reduce swelling and pain in the gums. The cream contains compounds called triterpenoids and flavonoids that help to soothe and calm the gums. When applied directly to the affected area, it can help to reduce redness and swelling. The cream also has antimicrobial properties, which help to prevent infection and promote healing. Additionally, calendula officinalis cream is gentle and non-irritating, making it a suitable treatment option for people with sensitive teeth and gums. Its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties work together to help alleviate swollen gums and promote a healthy mouth.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Ginkgo biloba herbal creams that help with swollen gums, you'll need dried Ginkgo biloba leaves and a few other simple ingredients. First, dry Ginkgo biloba leaves can be obtained from a health food store or online. Next, combine 2 tablespoons of dried Ginkgo biloba leaves with 1 cup of coconut oil in a small saucepan over low heat. Heat the mixture for 2-3 hours, stirring occasionally, until the oil has taken on a greenish color and the leaves are fully infused. Remove the saucepan from the heat and let it cool. Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean bowl, discarding the solids. Add 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of shea butter to the oil, and melt them in the microwave or in a double boiler. Once melted, mix the beeswax and shea butter with the infused oil and pour the mixture into a small container. Let the mixture cool and harden before using it as a cream to help reduce swollen gums. Apply a small amount to the affected area and gently massage it in.
remedies.image_alt: Calendula officinalis herbal creams for swollen gums

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus cream can be helpful for swollen gums because it has anti-inflammatory properties. This means it can reduce swelling and pain in the gums. Eucalyptus globulus is a type of plant that has been used for centuries in traditional medicine to treat various health issues, including mouth problems. When applied to swollen gums, the cream can help to calm the area and reduce discomfort. The eucalyptus oil in the cream also has antibacterial properties, which can help to fight off any infection that may be causing the swelling. By reducing swelling and fighting off infection, Eucalyptus globulus cream can help to promote healing and get rid of swollen gums. The cool and soothing sensation of the cream can also provide relief from the pain and discomfort associated with swollen gums.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Ginkgo biloba herbal cream for swollen gums, start by gathering the necessary ingredients: dried Ginkgo biloba leaves, coconut oil, beeswax, shea butter, and a few drops of tea tree oil. First, dry the Ginkgo biloba leaves in a low-temperature oven or by air-drying them, then grind them into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle. Next, melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl, then stir in the shea butter until it's fully incorporated. Once the mixture has cooled slightly, add the Ginkgo biloba powder and tea tree oil to the mixture and stir well. Pour the mixture into a small tin or jar and let it cool and harden completely before applying it to swollen gums. The Ginkgo biloba in the cream may help reduce inflammation and fight bacteria, while the coconut oil and shea butter provide moisturizing benefits to soothe and protect the gums.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, has been used for centuries to help with swollen gums. The active ingredients in ginger, like gingerol and shogaol, have anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and pain. When applied topically in a cream form, these ingredients can penetrate deep into the gums and surrounding tissues, helping to calm inflammation and ease discomfort. The anti-inflammatory properties in ginger cream can also help to reduce the risk of infection and promote healing in the affected area. This is especially helpful for people with gum disease or mouth sores, as it can help to soothe and protect the gums while they heal. Additionally, ginger has natural antibacterial properties that can help to fight off bacteria and other microorganisms that can cause infection and further inflammation.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Ginkgo biloba herbal cream for swollen gums, you'll need a few simple ingredients. First, you'll need to make a Ginkgo biloba infusion by mixing 2 tablespoons of dried Ginkgo biloba leaves with 1 cup of boiling water in a clean glass jar. Let it steep for 10-15 minutes, then strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into another container. Next, mix 1/2 cup of aloe vera gel and 1/4 cup of coconut oil in a bowl. Now, slowly add the Ginkgo biloba infusion to the aloe vera and coconut oil mixture, stirring well to combine. Finally, pour the mixture into a clean glass jar and let it cool to room temperature before sealing it. Your Ginkgo biloba herbal cream is now ready to use: apply a small amount to your swollen gums and gently massage it in several times a day to help reduce swelling and soothe discomfort.

remedies.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis cream is a natural remedy that can help with swollen gums. The cream contains compounds from the salvia plant, which have anti-inflammatory properties. When you apply the cream to your gums, these compounds can reduce swelling and ease pain. The anti-inflammatory properties in the cream work by blocking the chemicals that cause inflammation in the body. This can help to calm down the swollen gums and promote healing. The cream can also help to reduce bad breath and gum bleeding that often come with swollen gums. Regular use of the cream can help to prevent future gum problems. The anti-inflammatory properties in the cream can also help to reduce pain and discomfort associated with swollen gums. The cream is easy to apply and can be used as a part of your daily oral care routine.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Ginkgo biloba herbal creams that help with swollen gums, you will need dried Ginkgo biloba leaves, a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil, beeswax, and a few simple tools. First, combine 2 tablespoons of dried Ginkgo biloba leaves with 1 cup of carrier oil in a clean glass jar. Let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily, to allow the leaves to infuse their goodness into the oil. After the infusion is complete, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean jar, discarding the leaves. Next, melt 1-2 tablespoons of beeswax in a small saucepan over low heat, then slowly mix it with the infused oil, stirring constantly until the mixture thickens and cools. Pour the cream into small containers and let it cool completely before use. remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita, also known as peppermint, is a natural remedy that can help with swollen gums. When you apply a cream or ointment that contains peppermint oil, it helps to reduce swelling and ease pain in the gums. This is because peppermint has anti-inflammatory properties, which means it helps to calm down the body's natural response to injury or infection. The menthol in peppermint oil also helps to numb the area, making it feel cooler and more comfortable. When you apply peppermint cream to your gums, the menthol and other compounds in the oil can penetrate deep into the tissue, helping to break down any inflammation and reduce swelling. This can help to ease symptoms of swollen gums, such as redness, tenderness, and discomfort. By applying peppermint cream to your gums, you can help to create a soothing and calming environment that promotes healing and reduces pain.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Ginkgo biloba herbal cream for swollen gums, start by gathering the necessary ingredients: dried Ginkgo biloba leaves, coconut oil, beeswax, and a carrier oil like olive or jojoba oil. First, dry the Ginkgo biloba leaves in a low-temperature oven (150°F - 200°F) for 2-3 hours to remove any moisture. Then, grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or spice blender. In a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water, melt 1/4 cup of coconut oil and 2 tablespoons of beeswax. Once the mixture is fully melted, add 1-2 teaspoons of the ground Ginkgo biloba powder and 1-2 tablespoons of the carrier oil. Stir well to combine, then remove the bowl from the heat and let the mixture cool until it reaches a thick, creamy consistency. Pour the mixture into a clean container and let it cool completely before applying it to swollen gums to help reduce inflammation and promote healing.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba creams are a popular remedy for swollen gums. The active ingredients in Ginkgo biloba help to reduce inflammation and improve blood flow to the affected area. This increase in blood flow brings oxygen and nutrients to the gums, promoting healing and reducing swelling. Ginkgo biloba also has anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and calm the gums, reducing pain and discomfort. Additionally, Ginkgo biloba creams may help to reduce the risk of infection by promoting a healthy environment for the gums to recover. Some users also report that Ginkgo biloba creams help to reduce bleeding and gum recession, making it a valuable tool in oral health care.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Ginkgo biloba herbal creams for swollen gums, start by gathering the necessary ingredients: dried Ginkgo biloba leaves, carrier oil (like coconut or olive oil), beeswax, vitamin E oil, and a small saucepan. Begin by steeping 1-2 tablespoons of dried Ginkgo biloba leaves in 2 tablespoons of carrier oil for 2-3 hours or overnight. Strain the mixture using a cheesecloth or a coffee filter to get the infused oil. In a small saucepan, melt 2-3 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of the infused oil over low heat. Stir in 1 tablespoon of vitamin E oil and a few drops of any additional herbal essential oils you prefer. Continue heating and stirring until the mixture thickens and reaches a creamy consistency. Remove from heat and pour the mixture into a clean glass jar. Let it cool and solidify before using it to gently apply to swollen gums.
remedies.image_alt: Ginkgo biloba herbal creams for swollen gums

intro_desc: Swollen gums can be painful and uncomfortable, making it hard to eat, speak, and even smile. It's essential to address this issue because it can be a sign of a more serious problem like gum disease, which can lead to tooth loss and other health issues. Using herbal creams can be a natural and effective way to reduce swelling and promote healing. Echinacea purpurea is a key herb in these creams because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and ease pain. It also has antimicrobial properties, which help to fight off bacteria and other germs that can cause infection. Aloe barbadensis, also known as aloe vera, is another important herb in these creams. It has cooling and soothing properties that help to reduce inflammation and ease pain, making it an excellent remedy for swollen gums. Aloe vera also has antimicrobial properties, which help to prevent infection and promote healing. By using herbal creams that contain these herbs, you can help to reduce swelling, ease pain, and promote healing in your gums. This can improve your life by making it easier to eat, speak, and smile, and reducing your risk of more serious health problems.
supplementary_best: For swollen gums, a combination of Aloe barbadensis, Calendula officinalis, and Melaleuca alternifolia herbal creams is beneficial. Aloe barbadensis soothes and calms the gum tissue, reducing inflammation and discomfort. Calendula officinalis has antimicrobial properties that help prevent infections and promote healing in the gums. Melaleuca alternifolia, also known as tea tree oil, has antibacterial properties that target the bacteria causing the gum inflammation. The combination of these three herbs works well together because Aloe barbadensis provides a soothing effect, Calendula officinalis helps fight infections, and Melaleuca alternifolia targets the root cause of the inflammation. This combination is effective because it addresses the symptoms and the cause of swollen gums, making it a well-rounded remedy for this issue.
supplementary_avoid: If you have swollen gums, it's best to avoid herbal creams that can irritate or inflame the area further. One such herb to avoid is 'Capsicum annuum', which contains capsaicin, a compound that can increase blood flow and heat in the affected area, leading to more pain and discomfort. Another herb to be cautious with is 'Cinchona officinalis', which contains quinine, a compound that can cause allergic reactions and increase blood pressure, potentially worsening swollen gums. 'Glycyrrhiza glabra', also known as licorice root, should be used with caution as it can raise blood pressure and worsen gum inflammation. Similarly, 'Panax quinquefolius', or American ginseng, can increase blood pressure and worsen gum inflammation when used in excess. 'Urtica dioica', or stinging nettle, can cause skin irritation and allergic reactions, especially if you have sensitive skin. 'Valeriana officinalis', or valerian root, can interact with certain medications and worsen gum inflammation when used in combination with other herbs.
supplementary_other_remedies: Aloe vera gel can help reduce swelling in gums due to its anti-inflammatory properties. When applied directly to the gums, aloe vera gel can soothe and calm irritated tissues, reducing redness and swelling. The cooling sensation of aloe vera gel can also help to numb the pain caused by swollen gums.  Cold compresses or ice packs wrapped in a cloth can be applied to the affected area to reduce swelling. The cold temperature helps to constrict blood vessels, which in turn reduces blood flow to the area and minimizes swelling. This remedy can be especially helpful if you need to reduce swelling quickly.  Saltwater rinses are another effective remedy for swollen gums. Rinsing your mouth with warm saltwater several times a day can help to reduce swelling and ease pain. The antiseptic properties of salt help to kill bacteria that may be causing the inflammation, while the warm water helps to relax the gums and reduce tension.  Hydrogen peroxide mouthwashes can also be used to help reduce swelling in gums. However, it's essential to use the correct concentration and to rinse your mouth thoroughly after using the mouthwash. Hydrogen peroxide helps to kill bacteria and reduce inflammation, but it can be harsh on gums if used too frequently or in high concentrations.
supplementary_related_ailments: People with swollen gums may also experience pain, sensitivity, and difficulty eating or speaking. This is often caused by gum disease (periodontitis), which is linked to other health issues.  For example, research suggests that gum disease may contribute to heart disease, as bacteria from the mouth can enter the bloodstream and lead to inflammation in the heart. Medicinal herbs like turmeric and ginger have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce this inflammation.  Additionally, swollen gums may be a sign of diabetes, as people with diabetes are more prone to gum disease. The herb berberine has been shown to lower blood sugar levels and reduce inflammation, making it a potential treatment for both gum disease and diabetes.  Swollen gums can also be a symptom of digestive issues, such as irritable bowel syndrome (IBS). The herb slippery elm has anti-inflammatory properties and can help soothe digestive issues.  Furthermore, gum disease has been linked to respiratory problems like bronchitis and pneumonia, as bacteria from the mouth can enter the lungs and cause infections. The herb licorice root has anti-inflammatory properties and can help soothe respiratory issues.  It's essential to note that while medicinal herbs can be helpful in alleviating symptoms, they should not replace medical treatment. If you experience swollen gums or other symptoms, consult a healthcare professional for proper diagnosis and treatment.
